01.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Epiplastron. Top edge smooth curved, inner edges jagged suture. Curved groove line sweeping downwards to the right.

02.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Epiplastron. Top edge smooth curved, inner edges jagged suture. Curved groove line sweeping downwards to the left.

03.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Hyoplastron. Right edge straight vertical midline suture, left edge curved bridge suture, top inner corner notched. Horizontal texture with two parallel groove lines.

04.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Hyoplastron. Left edge straight vertical midline suture, right edge curved bridge suture, top inner corner notched. Horizontal texture with two parallel groove lines.

05.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Hypoplastron. Right edge straight vertical suture, top edge horizontal, bottom edge irregular. Single horizontal groove line forming backward-convex arc.

06.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Hypoplastron. Left edge straight vertical suture, top edge horizontal, bottom edge irregular. Single horizontal groove line forming backward-convex arc.

07.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Xiphiplastron. Top edge jagged suture, bottom tip concave or pointed. Groove line sloping steeply downwards to the left.

08.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Xiphiplastron. Top edge jagged suture, bottom tip concave or pointed. Groove line sloping steeply downwards to the right.

09.description: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Entoplastron. Rhomboid or diamond-shaped outline with jagged suture edges. Cross-shaped intersection of grooves on surface.
